mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and aggregate consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent signals an extended zero lower bound monetary regime that compresses short-term discount rates and suppresses volatility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm demand-driven expansion without labor market slack, which lifts corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signals of rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE fail to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of concurrent months.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals healthy real output growth without triggering Federal Reserve tightening expectations, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with CPIAUCSL growth below 2.5 percent and rising INDPRO do not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5 percent signals construction sector momentum and forward demand for durable goods and materials, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months when HOUST rises more than 5 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of at least 0.2 percentage points over three months signals tightening labor markets and accelerating wage income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55 percent of episodes where UNRATE declines by 0.2 percentage points or more over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirms a balanced demand-supply expansion where output keeps pace with spending, reducing margin squeeze fears and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with jointly rising RSAFS and INDPRO fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2 percent annualized signals durable aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth above 2 percent annualized does not associate with positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of quarters.
